subtopic: Geographical Features and Projects in India
title: Geographical Features in News
focus: Key natural geographical locations in India recently in the news, including mountain passes, glaciers, islands, peaks, forests, and seas.
rationale: This section covers distinct natural geographical features across India, making it a coherent cluster for geography-based map and fact questions.
sectionExcerpt: Covers Shipki La (Himachal Pradesh, Sutlej river), Gangotri Glacier (Uttarakhand, Bhagirathi river), Majuli Island (Brahmaputra, world's largest river island), Mt. Khangchendzonga (Sikkim, 3rd highest peak), Kancha Gachibowli Forest (Telangana), Kariyachalli Island (Gulf of Mannar), Barren Island Volcano (Andaman Sea, India's only active volcano), and the Andaman Sea (Seismic Zone V, Coral Triangle).
evidenceBullets: Shipki La is located in Kinnaur, Himachal Pradesh, on the India-Tibet border, where the Sutlej river enters India.
Majuli Island is the world's largest river island, formed by the Brahmaputra and Kherkutia Xuti/Subansiri rivers.
Mt. Khangchendzonga is the world's 3rd highest peak, located on the India-Nepal border in Sikkim.
Barren Island in the Andaman Sea is India's only active volcano.
Andaman Sea is part of Seismic Zone V and the Coral Triangle, bounded by Myanmar, Thailand, Malaysia, and Indonesia.

subtopic: Geographical Features and Projects in India
title: Rivers in News
focus: Indian rivers recently in the news due to water tribunals, hydroelectric projects, and natural disasters.
rationale: Groups all river-related news, focusing on origins, tributaries, and associated projects or tribunals, which is a classic UPSC geography topic.
sectionExcerpt: Details major rivers in the news: Beas (Ravi and Beas Waters Tribunal, joins Sutlej), Chenab (Sawalkot HEP, formed by Chandra and Bhaga), Subansiri (Lower Hydroelectric Project, largest Brahmaputra tributary), Kopili (NW-57, Meghalaya/Assam), Siyom (Arunachal Pradesh HEPs), Mandovi/Mahadayi (water tribunal, Dudhsagar falls), and Subarnarekha (flash floods, NW-96, Hundru Falls).
evidenceBullets: Beas River originates at Beas Kund (Rohtang Pass) and joins the Sutlej at Harike, Punjab.
Chenab River is formed by Chandra and Bhaga rivers and is the largest tributary of the Indus.
Subansiri River, known as the Gold River, is the largest tributary of the Brahmaputra.
Mandovi/Mahadayi River originates in Bhimgad (Western Ghats), flows through Goa, Karnataka, and Maharashtra, and features the Dudhsagar waterfall.
Subarnarekha River flows through Jharkhand, Odisha, and West Bengal, and hosts the Hundru Falls.

subtopic: Geographical Features and Projects in India
title: Infrastructure and Projects in News
focus: Major dams, hydroelectric projects, irrigation systems, and bridges in India.
rationale: This section shifts focus from purely natural features to human-made geographical and infrastructure projects, forming a distinct study block.
sectionExcerpt: Highlights key infrastructure projects: Ranjit Sagar Dam (Thein Dam) on the Ravi river (Punjab-J&K border), Etalin hydroelectric project on Dri and Tangon rivers (Dibang Valley, Arunachal Pradesh), Kaleshwaram Lift Irrigation Project (world's largest multi-stage lift irrigation on the Godavari river in Telangana), and the newly inaugurated Pamban Bridge connecting Rameswaram Island to mainland India.
evidenceBullets: Ranjit Sagar Dam (Thein Dam) is located on the Ravi River near the Punjab-Jammu & Kashmir border.
Etalin hydroelectric project is situated in the Dibang Valley, Arunachal Pradesh, on the Dri and Tangon rivers.
Kaleshwaram Lift Irrigation Project (KLIP) in Telangana is the world's largest multi-stage lift irrigation project, built on the Godavari River.
The new Pamban Bridge in Tamil Nadu replaces the original 2022-decommissioned cantilever bridge connecting Rameswaram Island to the mainland.
